clinic.ex: She works at a local medical clinic.

clinical.def: 臨床的、診所的、客觀冷靜的
clinical.ex: The drug is currently undergoing clinical trials.

clip.def: 夾子、迴紋針、片段、修剪、夾住、扣住、修剪、剪報
clip.col: paper clip, video clip, clip on

clone.def: 複製、複製人、無性繁殖、複製
clone.ex: Scientists succeeded in cloning a sheep.

closet.def: 壁櫥、更衣室、秘密的、把...關在房間裡、隱蔽

clothe.ex: The mountains were clothed in mist.

clothing.ex: She spends a lot of money on clothing.

clumsy.ex: That was a clumsy attempt to hide the truth.

clutch.def: 緊握、抓、離合器、(蛋的)一窩、緊握、抓住
clutch.col: clutch pedal, in the clutches of

coal.ex: Coal is used as a fuel.

coalition.ex: The two parties formed a coalition government.

coast.col: west coast, coastal area, along the coast

coastal.ex: The hurricane caused flooding in coastal regions.

coastline.ex: Scotland has a very rugged coastline.

cocaine.ex: He was arrested for possession of cocaine.

cock.def: 公雞、雌鳥、龍頭、首領、立起、使聳立、豎起

cocktail.ex: Would you like a cocktail before dinner?

cocoa.def: 可可粉、可可豆、可可飲料

coconut.ex: The island is covered with coconut trees.

cod.ex: We had grilled cod for dinner.

code.def: 密碼、代碼、準則、編碼、譯成密碼
code.col: postal code, area code, secret code, moral code

coexistence.ex: The two countries signed a treaty of peaceful coexistence.

coffee.col: coffee bean, coffee shop, instant coffee

coffin.ex: The coffin was made of dark oak.

coherent.def: 連貫的、一致的、條理清晰的
coherent.ex: The plan was not very coherent.

coil.ex: A snake was coiled on the rock.

coin.def: 硬幣、金屬錢幣、鑄造(硬幣)、創造(新詞)
coin.ex: He flipped a coin to decide.

coincide.ex: Our interests coincide in this matter.

coincidence.ex: It was just a pure coincidence that we met there.

cold.def: 冷的、寒冷的、冷淡的、感冒、寒冷、感冒地、冷淡地
cold.col: catch a cold, common cold, stone cold
cold.ex: The weather is very cold today.

collaborate.ex: Scientists from different countries collaborated on the project.

collaboration.ex: The two companies are in close collaboration.

collapse.def: 倒塌、崩潰、暴跌、倒下、瓦解、虛脫
collapse.ex: The building collapsed during the earthquake.

collar.col: white collar, blue collar, dog collar
collar.ex: The dog was wearing a leather collar.

colleague.ex: He is a colleague of mine from work.

collect.def: 收集、採集、募集、領取、收藏、收取的、由受話人付費的、由受話人付費地

collection.def: 收集、收藏品、徵收、祈禱文
collection.ex: The museum has a large collection of modern art.

collective.def: 集體的、共同的、集團、集體企業

collector.ex: He is a famous art collector.

college.ex: He is a student at the local college.

collide.ex: The two cars collided on the highway.

collision.ex: The collision resulted in several injuries.

colloquial.ex: He used a lot of colloquial expressions in his speech.

colon.ex: You should use a colon to introduce a list.

colonel.def: 陸軍(或空軍、陸戰隊)上校

colonial.def: 殖民地的、殖民時代的、殖民地居民

colonize.ex: The island was colonized by the British in the eighteenth century.

colony.def: 殖民地、僑民、群體(指生物)
colony.ex: The ant colony was under ground.

color.def: 顏色、色彩、顏料、臉色、給...著色、粉飾、變色、著色

column.ex: He writes a weekly column for the newspaper.

columnist.ex: She is a famous political columnist.

comb.def: 梳子、雞冠、梳頭、徹底搜尋、梳理
comb.col: comb the hair, comb for evidence
comb.ex: She combed her hair before going out.

combat.def: 戰鬥、格鬥、反對、與...戰鬥、對抗、戰鬥的
combat.ex: He saw combat in the war.

combination.ex: The dish is a perfect combination of sweet and spicy.

combine.def: 使結合、聯合、組合、康拜因(收割機)、集團
combine.ex: They combined forces to defeat the enemy.

combustible.def: 可燃的、易激動的、可燃物

comedy.ex: The show is a romantic comedy.

comfort.def: 舒適、安慰、慰問、撫慰、安慰
comfort.col: live in comfort, words of comfort

comma.ex: You should use a comma to separate items in a list.

command.ex: He has a good command of English.

commander.ex: The commander gave the order to attack.

commemorate.ex: The plaque commemorates the discovery of the site.

commence.ex: The meeting will commence at ten o'clock.

commencement.ex: The commencement ceremony will be held in May.

commend.ex: The teacher commended him for his hard work.

comment.def: 建議、評論、註解、評論、批評、發表意見、註解

commentary.ex: The sports announcer provided a running commentary on the game.

commentator.ex: He is a famous political commentator.

commerce.ex: The city is a major center of commerce.

commercial.def: 商業的、營利的、普通的、廣告
commercial.ex: The show was interrupted by several commercials.

commission.def: 佣金、委員會、委任、委任狀、委任、委託、任命

commit.def: 犯(罪)、承諾、委託、投入、自殺

commitment.ex: The company has a strong commitment to quality.

committee.ex: The committee is meeting to discuss the proposal.

commodity.ex: Oil is an important commodity in the world market.

common.def: 共同的、普通的、常見的、平民、公有地、普通地
common.col: common sense, common interest, in common
common.ex: The two brothers have a lot in common.

commonly.ex: This is a commonly used expression.

commonplace.def: 平凡的、陳腐的、老生常談、平常的事物

communal.ex: The kitchen and bathroom are communal.

communication.def: 溝通、通訊、通訊設施、信件、情報
communication.ex: Effective communication is key to success.

communicative.def: 愛說話的、交際的、溝通的
communicative.ex: She is a very communicative person.

communion.def: 共享、交往、交流、宗教教派、聖餐
communion.ex: They are in deep communion with nature.

communist.def: 共產主義的、共產主義者

community.col: local community, international community, academic community
community.ex: He is a well-known member of the local community.

commute.def: 通勤、變換、減刑、通勤路程
commute.ex: He commutes to work by train.

commuter.ex: The train is full of daily commuters.

compact.def: 緊湊的、精簡的、簡潔的、粉鏡、緊密的東西、使緊密、壓實、契約、協定
compact.ex: The computer is very compact and portable.

companion.ex: The dog is a faithful companion.

companionship.ex: She values the companionship of her friends.

company.col: keep company with, in the company of, insurance company

comparable.def: 可比較的、比得上的、相當的
comparable.ex: The two products are comparable in quality.

comparative.ex: We are living in comparative comfort.

compare.ex: If you compare the two, you will see the difference.

comparison.ex: The movie is good in comparison with the original book.

compartment.ex: Please store your bags in the overhead compartment.

compass.def: 指南針、羅盤、圓規、範圍、繞...而行、包圍、成就

compassion.ex: She felt a deep compassion for the homeless.

compassionate.ex: He is a very compassionate person.

compatible.ex: The software is compatible with Windows.

compel.ex: The rules compel us to wear uniforms.

compelling.def: 強大的、引人入勝的、強制的
compelling.ex: The evidence against him was compelling.

compensate.ex: The company promised to compensate the victims.

compensation.ex: The victims are seeking compensation for their losses.

compete.ex: The two teams are competing for the championship.

competence/competency.ex: The task is beyond his competence.

competent.def: 有能力的、勝任的、足夠的
competent.ex: She is a very competent manager.

competitive.def: 競爭性的、競爭激烈的、好勝的

compile.ex: The researchers are compiling a list of resources.

complain.ex: He is always complaining about the weather.

complaint.col: make a complaint, file a complaint
complaint.ex: She filed a complaint against her boss.

complement.def: 補足、補語、定額、補充、補足、與...相配
complement.ex: The wine is a perfect complement to the cheese.

complex.def: 複雜的、合成的、複合物、綜合設施、情結
complex.col: complex system, office complex, superiority complex

complexity.ex: The complexity of the system is overwhelming.

complexion.ex: She has a very clear complexion.

complication.ex: There were some complications during the surgery.

compliment.def: 致意、稱讚、恭維、稱讚、恭維、祝賀
compliment.ex: She paid me a nice compliment on my new dress.

complimentary.ex: Would you like a complimentary copy of the book?

comply.ex: You must comply with the rules.

component.def: 成分、零件、構成的、組成的

compose.ex: Water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen.

composite.def: 合成的、複合的、合成物、複合材料、使合成
composite.ex: The team created a composite image.

composition.def: 作曲、作文、成分、混合物、合成
composition.ex: The composition of the team has changed.

compound.def: 化合物、複合物、混合、結合、化合、增加、惡化、合成的、複合的

comprehend.ex: I fail to comprehend why he did it.

comprehension.ex: The mystery is beyond my comprehension.

comprehensive.def: 全面的、廣泛的、能理解的、綜合學校、綜合考試
comprehensive.ex: The study provides a comprehensive overview of the situation.

compress.def: 壓縮、精簡、壓敷料、冷敷膏
compress.ex: The gas is compressed for storage.

compromise.def: 妥協、讓步、危及、和解、折衷案、妥協
compromise.ex: We need to reach a compromise on this issue.

compulsion.ex: He felt a strong compulsion to tell the truth.

compulsory.ex: Attendance at the meeting is compulsory.

compute.ex: The computer computed the results in seconds.

computer.ex: I use a computer for my work.

comrade.ex: The two men were comrades during the war.

conceal.ex: She tried to conceal her disappointment.

concede.def: 承認、給予、讓步、(在選戰中)認輸
concede.ex: He finally conceded that he was wrong.

conceit.ex: I was shocked by his conceit.

conceited.ex: He is a very conceited young man.

conceivable.ex: We have tried every conceivable way to solve the problem.

conceive.ex: I can partically conceive of a world without war.

concentrate.def: 集中、濃縮、全神貫注、濃縮物
concentrate.ex: You must concentrate on your study.

concentration.def: 集中、全神貫注、濃度、集中營
concentration.ex: She has a very high level of concentration.

concept.ex: The theory is based on a basic concept.

conception.ex: He has no conception of the risk involved.

concern.col: major concern, express concern, of concern
concern.ex: The report expresses concern about the situation.

concerning.ex: He wrote a letter concerning the matter.

concert.def: 音樂會、一致、協調、協定、協調、齊力
concert.ex: The concert will be held next Saturday.

concess.ex: The government refused to concess to the protesters.

concession.def: 讓步、給予、特許權、租界、減價
concession.ex: The government made several concessions to the protesters.

concise.ex: Please give a concise answer to the question.

conclude.def: 締結、結束、斷定、完成、決定

conclusion.ex: We haven't reached a final conclusion yet.

condemn.def: 譴責、宣告有罪、判刑、宣告不合格
condemn.ex: The government condemned the terrorist attack.

condense.ex: The story was condensed for young readers.

condition.def: 條件、狀況、地位、健康、環境、使適應、以...為條件

condition.ex: The house is in good condition.

condole.ex: The neighbors came to condole with the family.
